title: Consciousness
author: Internet Encyclopedia of Philosophy
notes: IEP entry on consciousness — covers Nagel's subjective character argument, the hard problem, phenomenal vs access consciousness, and major positions. SEP Nagel entry is 'Not Yet Available'.

title: Consciousness
author: Robert Van Gulick
notes: SEP overview — covers phenomenal vs access consciousness, the hard problem, and major positions.

title: Facing Up to the Problem of Consciousness
author: David Chalmers
notes: Introduces the hard problem / easy problems distinction. Chalmers' own hosted HTML version.

title: Qualia
author: Michael Tye
notes: SEP entry on qualia — covers the knowledge argument (Mary's Room / Jackson), inverted qualia, absent qualia, and major responses. Replaces direct paper URL for Jackson which was JSTOR-blocked.

title: Physicalism
author: Daniel Stoljar
notes: SEP entry on physicalism — covers deflationary and eliminativist responses to qualia arguments. Essential materialist counterpoint to Chalmers/Nagel. Replaces Dennett 'Quining Qualia' (Tufts link now dead/redirects to homepage).

title: Functionalism
author: Janet Levin
notes: SEP entry on functionalism — covers multiple realisability, machine functionalism, and objections. Bridge to AI minds.

title: Computing Machinery and Intelligence
author: Alan Turing
notes: The Turing Test paper. UMBC hosted HTML version — verified accessible.

title: The Chinese Room Argument
author: David Cole
notes: SEP entry on the Chinese Room — covers Searle's original 1980 argument, all major objections (systems reply, robot reply, brain simulator reply) and responses. Replaces direct Cambridge URL for Searle 1980 which is login-gated despite claiming open-access.

title: Philosophy of Artificial Intelligence
author: Selmer Bringsjord
Naveen Govindarajulu
notes: SEP entry covering machine consciousness, strong AI, symbol grounding, and current debates.

title: The Turing Test
author: Graham Oppy
David Dowe
notes: SEP entry on the Turing Test — covers Turing's original proposal, objections (Searle, Lady Lovelace, etc.), and contemporary debates about machine intelligence and consciousness. Replaces Chalmers arXiv 2303.07103 which only has a 560-word abstract page available.
